title: Choose K from 1..N
description:
Given Generate all size-k combinations drawn from numbers 1..n in ascending lexicographic order. Return a list of lists. Use backtracking with an index and early pruning when remaining numbers are insufficient to fill the current path.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 20
- 0 ≤ k ≤ n
explanation:
Intuition
Each choice fixes the next number and recurses on the remainder of the range. Prune branches when not enough elements remain to complete a combination.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Track current path and next start index
2. Main Algorithm: Choose i in [start..n], append, recurse, then pop
3. Return Result: Add a copy of path when size hits k

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(C(n,k))
- Space Complexity: O(k) recursion

Key Insights
- Upper bound pruning reduces calls
- Paths are built in ascending order naturally

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to pop after recursion corrupts state
- Not handling k=0 should return [[]]
hints: Append a copy of path on success
